title: Agency in Language, Alane Suhr | Compile 26
summary: Alane Suhr examines how language models work and argues that agency in AI systems belongs to humans, not the models themselves. The talk covers instruction tuning, RLHF, and the meanings we attribute to words and AI concepts.
writeup: Alane Suhr walks through how language models function and makes the case that agency in AI systems rests with humans rather than the models. The talk covers instruction tuning and RLHF as the mechanisms that shape model behavior, and it examines the meanings people attach to words and AI concepts. The central takeaway: framing model outputs as evidence of agency misattributes to systems what is in fact a product of human choices.
